classification.cautionReason: Conciliar or creedal material received in mainstream Nicene Christian tradition, with denominational differences on some canons.

title: Canon XCIV.

verses.book: The Canons of the Council in Trullo.
verses.text: The canon subjects to penalties those who take heathen oaths, and we decree to them excommunication.

verses.book: The Canons of the Council in Trullo.
The Canons of the Council in Trullo.
verses.text: Whoever uses Gentile oaths, is worthy of punishment, for he is cut off.

verses.book: The Canons of the Council in Trullo.
verses.text: The reference is to canon lxxxj. of St. Basil’s canons.

verses.book: The Canons of the Council in Trullo.
verses.text: Tertullian (De Idolatria, cap. xx.) supposes that to swear by the false gods of the Gentiles, contains in itself some idolatry, an opinion shared by St. Basil, comparing those using such oaths with them who betrayed Christ, and who are partakers of the talk of devils.
